item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am amused by what you have here but I am simultaneously a bit disappointed by the fact that I feel you don't go far enough with the concept.

From the title, I expected a much greater, more vast city of whale carcasses and such, and throughout I kept thinking that what you had was that, just scaled way, way down.

+1, but with a wish for more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've read enough about the localized biomes associated with whalefalls to follow this easily. The animus against the boneworms was well depicted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two deep-sea surveillance drones are to actively monitor SCP-4607-A.

I do agree; this sounds a lot cleaner.

See more...
...see less.

The punctuated, short-spoken conversations lack a lot of personality

I guess that's the trade-off for readability which they formerly lack.

If these creatures speak through infrasonic vocalizations, aren't we just translating them into English? Why would be imbue gender, age, and language differences like Latin vs. English?

They don't necessarily speak. My thought is that they just emitting human conversations from an unknown medium. I included gender, age, and language to make them feel more human as it helped me imagine them during the drafting process.

Anyway, thanks for reading!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The miniature ecosystems within dead whales is a fascinating topic, and extending that to a social ecosystem is a clever concept.

Crit
Crit

That said, this ultimately falls flat for me. You overload the con-procs and description with a lot of needlessly bulky scientific terms.

"Two deep-sea surveillance drones are to be deployed on SCP-4607-A for daily monitoring purposes."
There's a lot of "word traffic" here. Why can't you just say "Two deep-sea surveillance drones are to actively monitor SCP-4607-A."?

The punctuated, short-spoken conversations lack a lot of personality, and you feel the need to pre-establish gendering and age-labeling, which feels unnecessary. The concept itself is also a bit muddled. If these creatures speak through infrasonic vocalizations, aren't we just translating them into English? Why would be imbue gender, age, and language differences like Latin vs. English?

Overall, novote for me. Keep at it though!

item.title: .au
item.content:encoded:
Sorry for the coldpost. This draft already had two critique threads (first, second) so I don't want to make a third. This was the original article before I deleted it. It's almost a total overhaul so I'm fairly confident to it now.

Image used is in the public domain because it contains materials that originally came from the U.S. National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.
